docs.title: web-tools
docs.text: Browser-based developer tools built with Lit Web Components.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: web-tools is a monorepo containing standalone, themeable web components for common developer tasks. Each tool works offline after initial load and can be embedded in any web page.

docs.title: Available Tools
docs.text: Tool Description Size Markdown Editor Markdown editor with live preview and PDF/DOCX export ~159KB JWT Editor JWT token decoder and inspector ~36KB Coordinate Picker Image-based SVG polygon coordinate picker ~43KB

docs.title: Using via CDN
docs.text:
Each tool is available as a standalone script:

<!-- JWT Editor -->
<script src="https://unpkg.com/@grokify/jwt-editor/dist/jwt-editor.min.js"></script>
<jwt-editor></jwt-editor>

docs.title: Using via npm
docs.text:
npm install @grokify/jwt-editor

import '@grokify/jwt-editor';

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Standalone - Each tool is a self-contained Web Component
  • Themeable - CSS custom properties for easy theming
  • Offline-capable - Works without network after initial load
  • Accessible - Built with accessibility in mind
  • Small footprint - Minimal bundle sizes

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• GitHub Repository
  • Getting Started
  • Theming Guide
  • Contributing

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to web-tools will be documented here.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial monorepo setup with pnpm workspaces
  • @grokify/markdown-editor - Markdown editor with live preview
  • Split-pane layout
  • GitHub Flavored Markdown support
  • PDF and DOCX export (optional)
  • Theming support
  • @grokify/jwt-editor - JWT token decoder
  • Automatic token parsing
  • Header/payload/signature display
  • Timestamp conversion
  • Validation with warnings/errors
  • Theming support
  • @grokify/coordinate-picker - SVG coordinate picker
  • Image upload (drag & drop)
  • Multi-shape support
  • Pin placement and sharing
  • Zoom controls
  • Live SVG preview
  • Theming support
  • E2E testing infrastructure with w3pilot
  • MkDocs documentation site
  • GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows

docs.title: Migration Notes
docs.text:
This release consolidates three tools into a single monorepo:

Previous Location New Package grokify/markdown-editor @grokify/markdown-editor grokify/jwt-editor @grokify/jwt-editor grokify/brandkit/tools @grokify/coordinate-picker

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide covers installation and basic usage of web-tools components.

docs.title: Via CDN (Recommended for Quick Start)
docs.text:
Add a script tag to load the component directly:

JWT EditorMarkdown EditorCoordinate Picker 
<script src="https://unpkg.com/@grokify/jwt-editor/dist/jwt-editor.min.js"></script>
<jwt-editor></jwt-editor>

<script src="https://unpkg.com/@grokify/markdown-editor/dist/markdown-editor.min.js"></script>
<markdown-editor></markdown-editor>

<script src="https://unpkg.com/@grokify/coordinate-picker/dist/coordinate-picker.min.js"></script>
<coordinate-picker></coordinate-picker>

docs.title: Via npm
docs.text:
Install the package you need:

# Install specific tools
npm install @grokify/jwt-editor
npm install @grokify/markdown-editor
npm install @grokify/coordinate-picker

Import in your JavaScript/TypeScript:

// Import registers the custom element automatically
import '@grokify/jwt-editor';

// Or import the class for advanced usage
import { JwtEditor } from '@grokify/jwt-editor';

docs.title: HTML
docs.text:
Simply add the custom element to your HTML:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <title>My App</title>
</head>
<body>
  <jwt-editor></jwt-editor>
  <script src="https://unpkg.com/@grokify/jwt-editor/dist/jwt-editor.min.js"></script>
</body>
</html>

docs.title: With Attributes
docs.text:
Configure components via attributes:

<!-- Dark theme (default) -->
<jwt-editor theme="dark"></jwt-editor>

<!-- Light theme -->
<jwt-editor theme="light"></jwt-editor>

<!-- With autosave -->
<jwt-editor autosave></jwt-editor>

docs.title: JavaScript API
docs.text:
Interact with components programmatically:

const editor = document.querySelector('jwt-editor');

// Set value
editor.value = 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...';

// Get current theme
console.log(editor.theme); // 'dark' or 'light'

// Toggle theme
editor.theme = editor.theme === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark';

docs.title: React
docs.text:
import '@grokify/jwt-editor';

function App() {
  return (
    <div>
      <jwt-editor theme="dark"></jwt-editor>
    </div>
  );
}

For TypeScript, add type declarations:

// custom-elements.d.ts
declare namespace JSX {
  interface IntrinsicElements {
    'jwt-editor': React.DetailedHTMLProps<
      React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement> & { theme?: 'dark' | 'light' },
      HTMLElement
    >;
  }
}

docs.title: Vue
docs.text:
<template>
  <jwt-editor :theme="theme"></jwt-editor>
</template>

<script setup>
import '@grokify/jwt-editor';
import { ref } from 'vue';

const theme = ref('dark');
</script>

docs.title: Svelte
docs.text:
<script>
  import '@grokify/jwt-editor';
  let theme = 'dark';
</script>

<jwt-editor {theme}></jwt-editor>

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Theming Guide - Customize colors and styles
  • Testing - Run automated tests
  • Contributing - Help improve web-tools

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to web-tools!

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Node.js 20+
  • pnpm 9+
  • Go 1.24+ (for E2E tests)

docs.title: Clone and Install
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/grokify/web-tools.git
cd web-tools
pnpm install

docs.title: Build
docs.text:
# Build all packages
pnpm build

# Build specific package
cd packages/jwt-editor
pnpm build

docs.title: Development Server
docs.text:
# Start dev server for a package
cd packages/jwt-editor
pnpm dev

# Or serve examples
npx serve examples -p 3000

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
web-tools/
├── packages/
│   ├── markdown-editor/     # @grokify/markdown-editor
│   ├── jwt-editor/          # @grokify/jwt-editor
│   └── coordinate-picker/   # @grokify/coordinate-picker
├── tests/
│   └── e2e/                 # w3pilot E2E tests
├── examples/                # Demo HTML pages
├── docs/                    # MkDocs documentation
└── shared/                  # Shared utilities (future)

docs.title: 1. Create a Branch
docs.text: git checkout -b feat/my-feature

docs.title: 2. Make Changes
docs.text: Edit files in the appropriate package under packages/.

docs.title: 3. Build and Test
docs.text:
# Build
pnpm build

# Manual testing
npx serve examples -p 3000
# Open http://localhost:3000/[tool].html

# E2E tests (if applicable)
cd tests/e2e
go test -tags=integration -v ./...

docs.title: 4. Commit
docs.text:
Use Conventional Commits:

# Feature
git commit -m "feat(jwt-editor): add copy button to header"

# Bug fix
git commit -m "fix(coordinate-picker): correct zoom calculation"

# Documentation
git commit -m "docs: update theming guide"

docs.title: 5. Push and Create PR
docs.text:
git push origin feat/my-feature
# Create PR on GitHub

docs.title: 1. Create Package Structure
docs.text: mkdir -p packages/my-tool/src/{components,utils,styles}

docs.title: 2. Create Configuration Files
docs.text:
package.json:

{
  "name": "@grokify/my-tool",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "dist/my-tool.umd.js",
  "module": "dist/my-tool.es.js",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "vite build && tsc --emitDeclarationOnly --declaration --outDir dist"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "lit": "^3.2.0"
  }
}

vite.config.ts:

import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
import { resolve } from 'path';

export default defineConfig({
  build: {
    lib: {
      entry: resolve(__dirname, 'src/index.ts'),
      name: 'MyTool',
      formats: ['es', 'umd', 'iife'],
      fileName: (format) => {
        if (format === 'iife') return 'my-tool.min.js';
        return `my-tool.${format}.js`;
      },
    },
  },
});

docs.title: 3. Create Component
docs.text:
// src/components/my-tool.ts
import { LitElement, html, css } from 'lit';
import { customElement, property } from 'lit/decorators.js';

@customElement('my-tool')
export class MyTool extends LitElement {
  @property({ type: String, reflect: true })
  theme: 'dark' | 'light' = 'dark';

  static override styles = css`
    :host {
      display: block;
      /* CSS custom properties for theming */
    }
  `;

  override render() {
    return html`<div>My Tool</div>`;
  }
}

docs.title: 4. Add Example
docs.text: Create examples/my-tool.html following the pattern of existing examples.

docs.title: 5. Add E2E Tests
docs.text: Create tests/e2e/my_tool_test.go.

docs.title: 6. Add Documentation
docs.text:
• Create docs/tools/my-tool.md
  • Update mkdocs.yml navigation

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• TypeScript - Use strict mode, proper types
  • Lit - Follow Lit best practices
  • CSS - Use CSS custom properties for theming
  • Go - Follow standard Go conventions

docs.title: Commit Message Guidelines
docs.text:
Type Description feat New feature fix Bug fix docs Documentation only style Formatting, no code change refactor Code restructuring test Adding/updating tests chore Maintenance tasks

Include scope when applicable:

feat(jwt-editor): add feature
fix(coordinate-picker): fix bug
docs(theming): update guide

docs.title: Questions?
docs.text:
• Open an issue on GitHub
  • Check existing issues and PRs

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
web-tools uses a multi-layer testing strategy to ensure reliability:

Layer Tool Purpose Unit Tests Vitest Test pure functions and utilities Component Tests @open-wc/testing Test Lit component behavior E2E Tests w3pilot Full browser integration tests

docs.title: Manual Testing
docs.text:
The fastest way to test during development:

# Build all packages
pnpm build

# Serve examples locally
npx serve examples -p 3000

# Open in browser
open http://localhost:3000/jwt-editor.html

docs.title: Run E2E Tests Locally
docs.text:
# Prerequisites: Chrome for Testing
cd tests/e2e
clicker install  # Install Chrome for Testing

# Start server in background
npx serve examples -p 3000 &

# Run tests
go test -tags=integration -v ./...

docs.title: E2E Testing with w3pilot
docs.text: web-tools uses w3pilot for end-to-end browser testing. w3pilot is a Go-based browser automation library that uses Chrome DevTools Protocol.

docs.title: Test Structure
docs.text:
tests/e2e/
├── go.mod                      # Go module for tests
├── e2e_test.go                 # Test setup and helpers
├── jwt_editor_test.go          # JWT Editor tests
├── coordinate_picker_test.go   # Coordinate Picker tests
└── markdown_editor_test.go     # Markdown Editor tests

docs.title: Writing E2E Tests
docs.text:
Basic test pattern:

//go:build integration

package e2e

import (
    "context"
    "testing"
    "time"

    "github.com/plexusone/w3pilot"
)

func TestJwtEditor_DecodeToken(t *testing.T) {
    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
    defer cancel()

    // Launch browser
    pilot, err := w3pilot.Browser.Launch(ctx, &w3pilot.LaunchOptions{
        Headless: true,
    })
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Failed to launch browser: %v", err)
    }
    defer func() { _ = pilot.Quit(ctx) }()

    // Navigate to component
    if err := pilot.Go(ctx, "http://localhost:3000/jwt-editor.html"); err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Failed to navigate: %v", err)
    }

    // Find and interact with elements
    textarea, err := pilot.Find(ctx, "textarea", nil)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Failed to find textarea: %v", err)
    }

    // Type JWT token
    if err := textarea.Type(ctx, "eyJhbGciOi...", nil); err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Failed to type: %v", err)
    }

    // Wait and verify
    time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)

    headerBadge, err := pilot.Find(ctx, ".badge-header", nil)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Token not decoded: %v", err)
    }
}

docs.title: Finding Elements
docs.text:
w3pilot supports multiple selector strategies:

// CSS selector
elem, _ := pilot.Find(ctx, "#my-id", nil)
elem, _ := pilot.Find(ctx, ".my-class", nil)
elem, _ := pilot.Find(ctx, "button[type='submit']", nil)

// Semantic selector (ARIA roles)
elem, _ := pilot.Find(ctx, "", &w3pilot.FindOptions{
    Role: "button",
    Text: "Submit",
})

// Combined
elem, _ := pilot.Find(ctx, "", &w3pilot.FindOptions{
    Role: "link",
    Text: "Learn More",
})

docs.title: Element Interactions
docs.text:
// Click
elem.Click(ctx, nil)

// Type text
elem.Type(ctx, "hello world", nil)

// Fill (clears first, then types)
elem.Fill(ctx, "new value", nil)

// Get text content
info := elem.Info()
text := info.Text

// Get attribute
value, _ := elem.Attribute(ctx, "theme")

docs.title: Testing Shadow DOM
docs.text:
w3pilot automatically pierces Shadow DOM boundaries, so you can find elements inside Web Components:

// This finds textarea inside jwt-editor's shadow DOM
textarea, _ := pilot.Find(ctx, "textarea", nil)

docs.title: CI Pipeline
docs.text:
E2E tests run automatically in GitHub Actions:

# .github/workflows/e2e-tests.yaml
name: E2E Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  e2e-tests:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Build components first
      - name: Build
        run: pnpm install && pnpm build

      # Install browser
      - name: Install Chrome
        run: clicker install

      # Start server
      - name: Start Server
        run: npx serve examples -p 3000 &

      # Run tests
      - name: Run E2E Tests
        run: |
          cd tests/e2e
          go test -tags=integration -v ./...

docs.title: Smoke Tests
docs.text:
Verify basic functionality:

  • Component loads without errors
  • Expected elements are present
  • Theme toggle works

docs.title: Interaction Tests
docs.text:
Test user workflows:

  • Input handling
  • Button clicks
  • Keyboard navigation

docs.title: Validation Tests
docs.text:
Test error handling:

  • Invalid input handling
  • Error message display
  • Edge cases

docs.title: Run with Debug Output
docs.text: W3PILOT_DEBUG=1 go test -tags=integration -v ./... -run TestJwtEditor

docs.title: Take Screenshots on Failure
docs.text:
func TestSomething(t *testing.T) {
    // ... test code ...

    if t.Failed() {
        pilot.Screenshot(ctx, "failure.png", nil)
    }
}

docs.title: Run Headed (Non-Headless)
docs.text: W3PILOT_HEADLESS=0 go test -tags=integration -v ./... -run TestJwtEditor

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use timeouts - Always use context with timeout
  2. Clean up - Use defer pilot.Quit(ctx) to close browser
  3. Wait for updates - Add small delays after interactions
  4. Check prerequisites - Verify server is running in TestMain
  5. Isolate tests - Each test should start fresh
  6. Use semantic selectors - Prefer ARIA roles over CSS when possible

docs.title: Adding New Tests
docs.text:
1. Create a new file: tests/e2e/{component}_test.go
  2. Add build tag: //go:build integration
  3. Import w3pilot and test package
  4. Write tests following patterns above
  5. Run: go test -tags=integration -v ./... -run TestNewComponent

docs.title: Theming
docs.text: All web-tools components support theming via CSS custom properties and a theme attribute.

docs.title: Theme Attribute
docs.text:
Toggle between light and dark modes:

<!-- Dark theme (default) -->
<jwt-editor theme="dark"></jwt-editor>

<!-- Light theme -->
<jwt-editor theme="light"></jwt-editor>

docs.title: JavaScript Toggle
docs.text:
const editor = document.querySelector('jwt-editor');

// Toggle theme
function toggleTheme() {
  editor.theme = editor.theme === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark';
}

docs.title: CSS Custom Properties
docs.text: Each component exposes CSS custom properties for customization.

docs.title: JWT Editor
docs.text:
jwt-editor {
  /* Backgrounds */
  --jwt-theme-bg-primary: #0a0e1a;
  --jwt-theme-bg-secondary: #1e293b;
  --jwt-theme-bg-tertiary: #334155;

  /* Text */
  --jwt-theme-text-primary: #f1f5f9;
  --jwt-theme-text-secondary: #94a3b8;
  --jwt-theme-text-muted: #64748b;

  /* Borders */
  --jwt-theme-border: #334155;

  /* Accent */
  --jwt-theme-accent: #06b6d4;
  --jwt-theme-accent-hover: #22d3ee;

  /* Status */
  --jwt-theme-error: #ef4444;
  --jwt-theme-warning: #f59e0b;
  --jwt-theme-success: #10b981;

  /* Fonts */
  --jwt-theme-font-sans: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
}

docs.title: Coordinate Picker
docs.text:
coordinate-picker {
  /* Backgrounds */
  --cp-theme-bg-primary: #1a1a2e;
  --cp-theme-bg-secondary: #2a2a4a;
  --cp-theme-bg-tertiary: #3a3a5a;

  /* Text */
  --cp-theme-text-primary: #eee;
  --cp-theme-text-secondary: #aaa;
  --cp-theme-text-muted: #888;

  /* Borders */
  --cp-theme-border: #4a4a8a;

  /* Accent */
  --cp-theme-accent: #4a4a8a;

  /* Fonts */
  --cp-theme-font-sans: system-ui, sans-serif;
}

docs.title: Markdown Editor
docs.text:
markdown-editor {
  /* Backgrounds */
  --mde-bg-primary: #0a0e1a;
  --mde-bg-secondary: #1e293b;
  --mde-bg-tertiary: #334155;

  /* Text */
  --mde-text-primary: #f1f5f9;
  --mde-text-secondary: #94a3b8;
  --mde-text-muted: #64748b;

  /* Accent */
  --mde-accent: #06b6d4;

  /* Fonts */
  --mde-font-sans: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
  --mde-font-mono: 'JetBrains Mono', 'Fira Code', monospace;
}

docs.title: PlexusOne Integration
docs.text:
When embedding in PlexusOne, map design tokens to component properties:

/* PlexusOne Design System to component mapping */
jwt-editor,
markdown-editor {
  --jwt-theme-bg-primary: var(--plexus-dark, #0a0e1a);
  --jwt-theme-bg-secondary: var(--plexus-slate, #1e293b);
  --jwt-theme-accent: var(--plexus-cyan, #06b6d4);
  --jwt-theme-text-primary: var(--plexus-text, #f1f5f9);

  --mde-bg-primary: var(--plexus-dark);
  --mde-accent: var(--plexus-cyan);
}

coordinate-picker {
  --cp-theme-bg-primary: var(--plexus-dark);
  --cp-theme-bg-secondary: var(--plexus-slate);
  --cp-theme-accent: var(--plexus-cyan);
}

docs.title: System Theme Detection
docs.text:
Detect and follow system theme preference:

function getSystemTheme() {
  return window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches
    ? 'dark'
    : 'light';
}

// Apply to component
const editor = document.querySelector('jwt-editor');
editor.theme = getSystemTheme();

// Watch for changes
window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')
  .addEventListener('change', (e) => {
    editor.theme = e.matches ? 'dark' : 'light';
  });

docs.title: Persist Theme Choice
docs.text:
// Load saved theme
const savedTheme = localStorage.getItem('theme') || 'dark';
editor.theme = savedTheme;

// Save on change
function setTheme(theme) {
  editor.theme = theme;
  localStorage.setItem('theme', theme);
}

docs.title: Brand Theme Example
docs.text:
/* Corporate blue theme */
.brand-theme jwt-editor {
  --jwt-theme-bg-primary: #001f3f;
  --jwt-theme-bg-secondary: #003366;
  --jwt-theme-accent: #0074d9;
  --jwt-theme-text-primary: #ffffff;
}

docs.title: High Contrast Theme
docs.text:
/* High contrast for accessibility */
.high-contrast jwt-editor {
  --jwt-theme-bg-primary: #000000;
  --jwt-theme-bg-secondary: #1a1a1a;
  --jwt-theme-text-primary: #ffffff;
  --jwt-theme-accent: #00ff00;
  --jwt-theme-border: #ffffff;
}

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use semantic names - Use --*-bg-primary not --*-dark-blue
  2. Provide fallbacks - Components have sensible defaults
  3. Test both themes - Verify light and dark modes
  4. Consider contrast - Ensure text is readable
  5. Be consistent - Use the same tokens across components

docs.title: Coordinate Picker
docs.text: A web component for picking coordinates from images to create SVG polygons.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Upload images (drag & drop or file picker)
  • Click to place coordinate pins
  • Multiple shape support (up to 20 polygons)
  • Pin sharing between shapes
  • Zoom controls (0.5x to 4x)
  • Live SVG preview
  • Copy individual polygon or full SVG
  • Dark and light theme support

docs.title: Installation
Basic
docs.text: <coordinate-picker></coordinate-picker>

docs.title: With Options
docs.text:
<!-- Light theme -->
<coordinate-picker theme="light"></coordinate-picker>

<!-- Custom number of shapes -->
<coordinate-picker num-shapes="6"></coordinate-picker>

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Type Default Description theme 'dark' | 'light' 'dark' Color theme num-shapes number 4 Initial number of shape slots

docs.title: Events
docs.text:
// Fired when SVG output changes
picker.addEventListener('svg-generated', (e) => {
  console.log(e.detail.svg);
});

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Load Image - Click the upload area or drag & drop an image
  2. Select Shape - Click a shape card to make it active
  3. Place Pins - Click on the image to place coordinate pins
  4. Switch Shapes - Click another shape card to add pins to it
  5. Share Pins - Click an existing pin to add it to the current shape
  6. Copy Output - Click polygon code or full SVG to copy

docs.title: Styling
docs.text:
coordinate-picker {
  /* Backgrounds */
  --cp-theme-bg-primary: #1a1a2e;
  --cp-theme-bg-secondary: #2a2a4a;

  /* Text */
  --cp-theme-text-primary: #eee;
  --cp-theme-accent: #4a4a8a;

  /* Borders */
  --cp-theme-border: #4a4a8a;
}

docs.title: Adding Pins
docs.text:
• Click on the image to add a new pin
  • The pin is automatically added to the active shape

docs.title: Reusing Pins
docs.text:
• Click an existing pin to open the context menu
  • Select "Add to Shape X" to share the pin

docs.title: Removing Pins
docs.text:
• Click a pin to open the context menu
  • Select "Remove from Shape X" or "Delete Pin"

docs.title: Shape Colors
docs.text:
Shapes are assigned colors in order:

Shape Color 1 Red (#e74c3c) 2 Blue (#3498db) 3 Green (#2ecc71) 4 Orange (#f39c12) 5 Purple (#9b59b6) 6+ Cycles through palette

docs.title: SVG Output
docs.text:
The component generates clean SVG code:

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 300">
  <rect width="400" height="300" fill="#000000"/>
  <polygon points="10,20 50,60 90,20" fill="#00FF00"/>
  <polygon points="100,120 150,160 190,120" fill="#00FF00"/>
</svg>

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <title>SVG Coordinate Picker</title>
  <style>
    body {
      margin: 0;
      background: #1a1a2e;
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <coordinate-picker theme="dark"></coordinate-picker>
  <script src="https://unpkg.com/@grokify/coordinate-picker/dist/coordinate-picker.min.js"></script>
  <script>
    document.querySelector('coordinate-picker')
      .addEventListener('svg-generated', (e) => {
        console.log('SVG:', e.detail.svg);
      });
  </script>
</body>
</html>

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Creating image maps
  • Defining clickable regions
  • Building SVG masks
  • Tracing shapes from reference images
  • Game development (collision shapes)

docs.title: Guide: Cleaning Up Coordinates
docs.text: When tracing logos or precise geometric shapes, raw click coordinates are approximate. This guide shows how to refine them into exact geometry.

docs.title: Step 1: Trace the Shape
docs.text:
Use the Coordinate Picker to click approximate points on your reference image. Don't worry about precision—just capture the general shape.

Example raw output:

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 400">
  <rect width="400" height="400" fill="#000000"/>
  <polygon points="199,52 328,126 265,162 135,88" fill="#00FF00"/>
  <polygon points="265,162 199,199 328,272 329,200" fill="#00FF00"/>
  <polygon points="199,199 134,237 69,198 70,126" fill="#00FF00"/>
  <polygon points="134,237 262,310 200,347 70,272" fill="#00FF00"/>
</svg>

docs.title: Step 2: Analyze the Geometry
docs.text:
Look for patterns in your coordinates:

  1. X-coordinate clusters - Find repeating X values (e.g., 70, 135, 200, 265, 330)
  2. Spacing - Calculate the interval between clusters (e.g., 65 units)
  3. Diagonal slopes - Measure rise/run for diagonal lines (e.g., 37/65 ≈ 0.569)
  4. Vertical edges - Identify lines that should be exactly vertical

docs.title: Step 3: Build a Coordinate Grid
docs.text:
Create a reference grid based on your analysis:

Property Value X-coordinates 70, 135, 200, 265, 330 (spacing: 65) Diagonal slope ±37/65 (≈0.569) Vertical edges x=70 and x=330 Y-step per X-unit 37 per 65 units

docs.title: Step 4: Snap to Grid
docs.text:
Rewrite coordinates using exact values from your grid:

Cleaned output:

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 400">
  <rect width="400" height="400" fill="#000000"/>
  <!-- Shape 1: Top center -->
  <polygon points="200,50 330,124 265,161 135,87" fill="#00FF00"/>
  <!-- Shape 2: Right side (vertical edge at x=330) -->
  <polygon points="265,161 200,198 330,272 330,198" fill="#00FF00"/>
  <!-- Shape 3: Left side (vertical edge at x=70) -->
  <polygon points="200,198 135,235 70,198 70,124" fill="#00FF00"/>
  <!-- Shape 4: Bottom center -->
  <polygon points="135,235 265,309 200,346 70,272" fill="#00FF00"/>
</svg>

docs.title: Step 5: Verify Shared Vertices
docs.text:
Ensure shapes that touch share exact coordinates:

Vertex Shared By (265, 161) Shape 1 ↔ Shape 2 (200, 198) Shape 2 ↔ Shape 3 (135, 235) Shape 3 ↔ Shape 4

docs.title: Tips for Clean Geometry
docs.text:
• Parallelograms: Opposite sides must have identical slope
  • Vertical lines: All points share the same X value
  • Horizontal lines: All points share the same Y value
  • Shared edges: Adjacent shapes must use identical point coordinates
  • Symmetry: Use a center axis and mirror coordinates

docs.title: Common Patterns
docs.text:
Isometric shapes (30° angles):

  • Slope: ±0.577 (1/√3)
  • Y-step: 57.7 per 100 X-units

45° angles:

  • Slope: ±1.0
  • Y-step: 100 per 100 X-units

Hexagonal shapes:

  • Interior angles: 120°
  • Slope: ±1.732 (√3)

docs.title: JWT Editor
docs.text: A web component for decoding and inspecting JWT tokens.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Decode JWT tokens instantly
  • View header, payload, and signature sections
  • Automatic timestamp conversion (iat, exp, nbf)
  • Validation status with warnings and errors
  • Dark and light theme support
  • Copy decoded JSON to clipboard
  • Autosave option for persistence

docs.title: Installation
Basic
docs.text: <jwt-editor></jwt-editor>

docs.title: With Options
docs.text:
<!-- Light theme -->
<jwt-editor theme="light"></jwt-editor>

<!-- With autosave to localStorage -->
<jwt-editor autosave></jwt-editor>

<!-- Pre-filled value -->
<jwt-editor id="editor"></jwt-editor>
<script>
  document.getElementById('editor').value = 'eyJhbGciOi...';
</script>

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Type Default Description theme 'dark' | 'light' 'dark' Color theme autosave boolean false Persist token in localStorage value string '' JWT token to decode

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
interface JwtEditor {
  theme: 'dark' | 'light';
  autosave: boolean;
  value: string;
}

docs.title: Styling
docs.text:
jwt-editor {
  /* Backgrounds */
  --jwt-theme-bg-primary: #0a0e1a;
  --jwt-theme-bg-secondary: #1e293b;

  /* Text */
  --jwt-theme-text-primary: #f1f5f9;
  --jwt-theme-accent: #06b6d4;

  /* Status colors */
  --jwt-theme-error: #ef4444;
  --jwt-theme-warning: #f59e0b;
  --jwt-theme-success: #10b981;
}

docs.title: Token Validation
docs.text:
The component validates:

Check Level Description Format Error Must have 3 dot-separated parts Algorithm Warning Warns if alg: none Expiration Error Checks if token has expired Not Before Error Checks if token is not yet valid Issued At Warning Warns if iat is in the future

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
Client-Side Only

The JWT is decoded entirely in the browser. No data is sent to any server.

No Signature Verification

This tool decodes JWTs for inspection only. It does not verify signatures.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <title>JWT Decoder</title>
  <style>
    body {
      margin: 0;
      min-height: 100vh;
    }
    jwt-editor {
      height: 100vh;
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <jwt-editor theme="dark"></jwt-editor>
  <script src="https://unpkg.com/@grokify/jwt-editor/dist/jwt-editor.min.js"></script>
</body>
</html>

docs.title: Markdown Editor
docs.text: A client-side Markdown editor with live preview and export capabilities.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Split-pane editor and preview layout
  • GitHub Flavored Markdown support
  • Live preview as you type
  • PDF export (optional dependency)
  • DOCX export (optional dependency)
  • Syntax highlighting in code blocks
  • Autosave to localStorage
  • Dark and light theme support

docs.title: Installation
Optional Dependencies
docs.text:
For PDF/DOCX export, install peer dependencies:

# PDF export
npm install html2pdf.js

# DOCX export
npm install docx

docs.title: Basic
docs.text: <markdown-editor></markdown-editor>

docs.title: With Options
docs.text:
<!-- Light theme -->
<markdown-editor theme="light"></markdown-editor>

<!-- Pre-filled content -->
<markdown-editor id="editor"></markdown-editor>
<script>
  document.getElementById('editor').setValue('# Hello World');
</script>

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Type Default Description theme 'dark' | 'light' 'dark' Color theme

docs.title: Properties & Methods
docs.text:
interface MarkdownEditor {
  theme: 'dark' | 'light';

  // Get/set markdown content
  getValue(): string;
  setValue(markdown: string): void;

  // Export (requires optional dependencies)
  exportPDF(): Promise<void>;
  exportDOCX(): Promise<void>;
}

docs.title: Events
docs.text:
// Content changed
editor.addEventListener('change', (e) => {
  console.log(e.detail.value);
});

// Export completed
editor.addEventListener('export', (e) => {
  console.log(e.detail.format); // 'pdf' or 'docx'
});

docs.title: Styling
docs.text:
markdown-editor {
  /* Backgrounds */
  --mde-bg-primary: #0a0e1a;
  --mde-bg-secondary: #1e293b;

  /* Text */
  --mde-text-primary: #f1f5f9;
  --mde-accent: #06b6d4;

  /* Fonts */
  --mde-font-sans: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
  --mde-font-mono: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
}

docs.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
docs.text: Shortcut Action Ctrl+B Bold Ctrl+I Italic Ctrl+K Link Ctrl+S Save (triggers autosave)

docs.title: Markdown Support
docs.text:
The editor supports:

  • Headings (# to ######)
  • Bold and italic
  • Links and
  • Inline code and code blocks
  • Lists (ordered and unordered)
  • Blockquotes
  • Tables
  • Task lists
  • Strikethrough
  • Syntax highlighting

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <title>Markdown Editor</title>
  <style>
    body {
      margin: 0;
      min-height: 100vh;
    }
    markdown-editor {
      height: 100vh;
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <markdown-editor theme="dark"></markdown-editor>
  <script src="https://unpkg.com/@grokify/markdown-editor/dist/markdown-editor.min.js"></script>
  <script>
    const editor = document.querySelector('markdown-editor');

    // Set initial content
    editor.setValue(`# Welcome

Start typing your markdown here!

- Supports **bold** and *italic*
- Code blocks with syntax highlighting
- And much more...
`);
  </script>
</body>
</html>

docs.title: PDF Export
docs.text:
To enable PDF export:

  1. Install html2pdf.js: 

    npm install html2pdf.js

  2. Import before using: 

    <script src="https://unpkg.com/html2pdf.js/dist/html2pdf.bundle.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://unpkg.com/@grokify/markdown-editor/dist/markdown-editor.min.js"></script>

  3. Use export button or API: 

    editor.exportPDF();

docs.title: DOCX Export
docs.text:
To enable DOCX export:

  1. Install docx: 

    npm install docx

  2. Use export API: 

    editor.exportDOCX();
